analysis.consistency: High. The data fields appear consistent with standard spell descriptions (name, level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, description, and class list).
analysis.quality: High. The description is clear and adheres to standard D&D 5e terminology ('AC can't be less than 16', 'Concentration').

mechanics.core_effect: Sets a minimum Armor Class (AC) of 16 for the target, overriding lower AC values derived from armor or lack thereof.
constraints: Requires Verbal, Somatic, and Material components (Material component is consumed or expended, depending on interpretation, but listed as 'a handful of oak bark').
mechanics.synergy: Potentially very strong for low-AC targets (like unarmored casters or those wearing light armor). It does not stack with Mage Armor (which sets AC to 13 + Dex modifier) if the resulting AC is lower than 16.
mechanics.balance_check: Level 2 spell seems appropriate for a guaranteed minimum AC of 16, given the concentration requirement and the fact that higher-level spells offer better static AC bonuses or temporary hit points.
